items.title: Northern Territory musicians escape to international festival in the UK

children.content: Northern Territory musicians attending The Great Escape music festival in the UK are hoping their experience will open more doors for regional artists.

items.title: Outback cattle station granted permit to cull 20,000 native birds

children.content: A permit to cull 20,000 native birds has been granted to a cattle station in the Northern Territory in response to an "overpopulation" of destructive and "exceptionally noisy" galahs and little corellas.

image.altText: A flying pink and grey galah in soft light.

items.title: What we know about the NT government's plan to change child protection laws

children.content: Here's what you need to know about the proposed changes to the NT child protection legislation, introduced into parliament on Wednesday.

image.altText: A pink mural on a brick wall of a child holding an adult's hand.

items.title: NT government accused of 'politicising tragedy' with child protection laws

children.content: Peak Aboriginal bodies have accused the Northern Territory government of using Kumanjayi Little Baby's alleged murder as political cover to push ahead with sweeping changes to child protection laws.

articles.title: NT government announces details of review into Kumanjayi Little Baby's situation

children.content: The NT government has revealed the scope of a review into the territory's child protection system, and announced that it will be led by retired NSW police commissioner Karen Webb and long-standing territory public servant Greg Shanahan.

items.title: Runner takes on mountainous 231km to honour son's memory

children.content: Running hundreds of kilometres up and down the Larapinta Trail is a gruelling challenge most people will never attempt. But for Ben Mack, completing it would be a dream come true.

image.altText: A man wearing a legionnaires cap leans against a gum tree trunk.

items.title: NT Police fraud squad seizes grenades, warheads in raids south of Darwin

children.content: Northern Territory Police have seized 32 explosive ordnance items, including hand grenades, rocket components and warheads in raids south of Darwin.

image.altText: A collection of military ordnance.

items.title: Man on mobility scooter killed by car in Alice Springs

children.content: A 49-year-old man using a mobility scooter has died after being hit by a car on a pedestrian crossing in Alice Springs.

image.altText: NT Police car flashing lights

items.title: 'Something catastrophic is going to happen': NT mayor on car thefts spike

children.content: Katherine's mayor says residents feel less safe than they did a year ago and is calling on Northern Territory Chief Minister Lia Finocchiaro to hold an "urgent" roundtable on crime in the Top End town.

image.altText: A woman looking over floodwaters with a concerned expression on her face.

items.title: The food, music and praise served up at the Trump-Xi state banquet

children.content: Guests dined on Beijing roast duck and tiramisu while the People's Liberation Army band performed some classic hits.

articles.title: The key takeaways from Trump and Xi's meeting in China

children.content: From a red carpet welcome to a high-profile tech entourage and a pointed reference to Taiwan, here are the main takeaways from Donald Trump's meeting with Xi Jinping in China.

articles.title: Who is in Trump's entourage of billionaires and executives in Beijing?

children.content: US President Donald Trump has brought a delegation of business executives for his visit to Beijing. Here's why they made the trans-Pacific trip.

items.title: Delta Goodrem reaches Eurovision grand final after fiery performance

children.content: Delta Goodrem has performed her pop ballad Eclipse at Eurovision in Vienna, the 11th time Australia has appeared in the musical extravaganza.

items.title: Soon after John quit the Liberal Party, the strange phone calls started

children.content: When John Ternel quit the Victorian Liberal Party this week the party mistakenly sent an email to members saying he had died. Mr Ternel says the ordeal confirmed to him that the Victorian Liberals are in shambles.

image.altText: A man in a light business shirt and dark jacket looks into camera, with office buildings behind him.

items.title: Overdose at Crown Melbourne every two weeks, data shows

children.content: Paramedics have attended more than 50 suspected overdoses at Crown Melbourne in the past two years, with the casino complex averaging an ambulance call-out for a suspected overdose every two weeks, data released exclusively to the ABC shows.

image.altText: One of the main entrances to Crown Casino in Melbourne.

items.title: How the wisdom of experience won the State of Origin series for New South Wales

children.content: New South Wales series win over Queensland proves that sometimes, like anything else, practice makes perfect in State of Origin football.

articles.title: Miraculous try-savers hand NSW second straight Origin series win

children.content: For the second straight year, NSW secures the Women's State of Origin shield in Game II, with Jesse Southwell and Teagan Berry coming up with stunning try-savers to get the Blues over the line.

items.title: Archaeologists restoring oldest colonial-era boat ever found in Australia

children.content: Maritime archaeologists are working to restore the oldest colonial-era boat ever found in Australia, discovered during the construction of the Barangaroo metro station in 2018.

image.altText: Archaeologists wearing high-vis and protective gear working together to excavate colonial-era boat.

items.title: 'Mega' gas-fired power stations could be the new normal, analyst says

children.content: Energy Australia says the proposed 1.4-gigawatt gas-fired power plant would be critical to the energy transition by supplying reliable back-up power, but analysts have flagged supply and economic issues.

items.title: Sexual abuse, a thwarted Olympic dream, and the sister who uncovered the truth

items.title: 'Different doctor every fortnight' is the reality for these residents

items.title: Abandoned base's toxic waste clean-up deadline rejected

children.content: The Australian government has officially ruled out cleaning up Antarctica's contaminated Wilkes Station before the end of the decade, as previously recommended by a federal parliamentary committee.

items.title: Antarctic expeditioner removed after weapon incident

children.content: A member of an Antarctic expedition who allegedly threatened colleagues with a bladed weapon has been removed from a Korean research station in an emergency evacuation.

items.title: Entire university sector accused of antisemitism failure

items.title: ICC fugitive flees Philippine Senate after shooting chaos

children.content: Senator Ronald dela Rosa, who is wanted by the International Criminal Court on charges of crimes against humanity, had been holed up inside the Philippine Senate since Monday to avoid arrest. His whereabouts is now unknown.

items.title: Research links seismic testing to decreased commercial fish catch

items.title: Why four lanes might not be better than two on this Canberra road

children.content: The latest federal budget has committed $50 million to duplicate a road in West Belconnen, but transport planning experts warn it could encourage even more peak-hour traffic.

items.title: Email reveals Jewish group warned police about Bondi gunman in 2019

children.content: A Jewish security agency raised concerns about Bondi Beach gunman Naveed Akram years before the terror attack that left 15 people dead, a document shows.
